data.body:
Addendum — additional failure mode (caveat 1):

### Actual Behavior (additional)

Brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a invokes `preconditions-record.sh` with `2>/dev/null || true`:

```bash
.claude/scripts/dso preconditions-record.sh \
  --ticket-id "$epic_id" \
  --gate-name "brainstorm_complete" \
  --session-id "${SESSION_ID:-unknown}" \
  --tier "minimal" 2>/dev/null || true

.claude/scripts/dso ticket untag <epic-id> scrutiny:pending
.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag <epic-id> brainstorm:complete
```

If `preconditions-record.sh` errors for any reason (script bug, permission issue, transient ticket-store state, future schema rejection), brainstorm silently continues, untags `scrutiny:pending`, and tags `brainstorm:complete` — producing the same tag-without-event drift this bug describes. There is no verification that the record actually landed before the tag transition. The producer (brainstorm) and consumer (preplanning gate) can drift even on epics processed after `ee92-7fb9` (the commit that added Step 3a), without surfacing any signal.

This is distinct from caveats 2 (validator exit-2 with no auto-locator) and 3 (migration shim writes tag without preconditions). Together the three modes form a tag/event drift cycle: producer can fail silently (caveat 1), consumer cannot find an event that exists (caveat 2), and the historical-migration path doesn't write the event at all (caveat 3).

### Fix surface (d)

Remove the `2>/dev/null || true` swallow from brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a. Either:
- (d.1) Let `preconditions-record.sh` failures abort the brainstorm Phase 3 Step 3a sequence (do not tag `brainstorm:complete` if the event was not recorded — preserves the producer/consumer contract).
- (d.2) Capture stderr to a log + emit a visible WARN to the user, then continue with tagging only if the user acknowledges. Avoids hard-blocking on transient issues but surfaces the drift.

(d.1) is the stricter contract; (d.2) preserves graceful-degradation while removing silence.
